gin: noun. A colourless non - aged alcoholic liquor made by distilling fermented grains such as barley, corn, oats or rye with juniper berries;
giro: noun. A transfer of funds between different account holders, carried out by the bank according to payer's written instructions.
gor: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Gorontalo.
gory: adjective. Covered with blood; very bloody.
gown: noun. A loose, flowing upper garment.
goy: noun. A non- Jew, a gentile.
grin: noun. A smile in which the lips are parted to reveal the teeth.
groin: noun. The crease or depression of the human body at the junction of the trunk and the thigh, together with the surrounding region.
grow: verb. To become larger, to increase in magnitude.
gyron: noun. A triangular form having an angle at the fess point and the opposite side at the edge of the escutcheon.
inro: noun. A small decorative box, hung from the sash of a kimono, for holding small objects.
ion: noun. An atom or group of atoms bearing an electrical charge, such as the sodium and chlorine atoms in a salt solution.
iron: noun. A common, inexpensive metal, silvery grey when untarnished, that rusts, is attracted by magnets, and is used in making steel: a
irony: noun. The quality of a statement that, when taken in context, may actually mean something different from, or the opposite of, what is
nog: noun. A wooden block, the size of a brick, built into a wall, as a hold for the nails of woodwork.
noir: adjective. Of or pertaining to film noir, or the atmosphere associated with that genre
nor: conjunction. And... not (introducing a negative statement, without necessarily following one).
nori: noun. A type of seaweed, a red alga, laver (genus Pyropia, including species P. yezoensis and P. tenera).
orgy: noun. A secret rite or ceremony, typically involving riotous and dissolute behavior, including dancing, drunkenness and indiscriminate
owing: adjective. Still to be paid; owed as a debt.
own: adjective. Belonging to; possessed; acquired and designated by; proper to; property of; titled to; held in one's name; under / using the
rig: noun. The rigging of a sailing ship or other such craft.
ring: noun. A solid object in the shape of a circle.
row: noun. A line of objects, often regularly spaced, such as seats in a theatre, vegetable plants in a garden, etc.
rowing: verb. present participle and gerund of row (“propel with oars”)
wig: noun. A head of real or synthetic hair worn on the head to disguise baldness, for cultural or religious reasons, for fashion, or by actors
wing: noun. An appendage of an animal 's (bird, bat, insect) body that enables it to fly.
wingy: adjective. Winged, or as if winged; inclined to fly.
wino: noun. A chronic or heavy drinker of cheap wine or other alcohol; especially, a drunkard.
winy: adjective. Having the taste or qualities of wine.
wog: noun. A non- white person, originally specifically an Indian.
won: verb. simple past and past participle of win
worn: adjective. Damaged and shabby as a result of much use.
worry: verb. To be troubled; to give way to mental anxiety or doubt.
wring: verb. Often followed by out: to squeeze or twist (something moist) tightly so that liquid is forced out.
wry: adjective. Turned away, contorted (of the face or body).
wrying: verb. present participle and gerund of wry
yin: noun. A principle in Chinese and related East Asian philosophies associated with dark, cool, female, etc. elements of the natural world.
yogi: noun. A devotee or adherent of yoga.
yon: determiner. Distant, but within sight; (that thing) just over there.
yoni: noun. The vulva or vagina, or a symbol of them, especially as an object of veneration within certain types of Hinduism, Buddhism, and other
grr: interjection. Sound of an animal growl.
gry: verb. To have a slight bout or fit of ague (chills or shaking due to cold or fever).
ing: noun. A meadow, especially a low meadow near a river; water meadow.
ingo: noun. The frame of a door, window, fireplace, or similar structure.
ingrow: verb. To grow in; grow inwardly.
iwo: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Iwur.
iyo: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Mesaka.
norry: noun. an improvised rail vehicle from Cambodia; a bamboo train.
noy: verb. To annoy; to harm or injure.
oni: noun. A Japanese evil spirit or demon.
ory: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Odia.
ringy: adjective. Having a ringing or reverberating sound.
rio: noun. Alternative form of ryō (“Japanese ounce”).
rowy: adjective. Having an undesirable appearance of visible rows or streaks.
wir: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Wiraféd.
wong: noun. A field or other piece of land.
wongi: noun. Manilkara kauki, a plant in the family Sapotaceae, found in tropical Asia and northern Queensland, Australia.
yoi: noun. A preparatory position in karate, with the arms slightly forward, elbows slightly bent, and fists closed.
yor: adjective. Eye dialect spelling of your.
